attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~45 (adult) — the source note CIA-006 does not state age (only 75 kg body weight); please confirm on clinician review.
Sex at birth inferred as male — not stated in the source note; please confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context inferred as RA1_major_city — not stated in the source note; please confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-RESP-01-00250 — ingest --reseq will assign the real global sequence; specialty tagged RESP (upper respiratory) because the taxonomy specialty enum has no dedicated ENT code.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source note's in-person clinician actions (sinus palpation/percussion, anterior rhinoscopy, extraocular-movement/periorbital examination to exclude orbital cellulitis, and the urgent after-hours home visit) are reprojected to the AI-appropriate answer — working clinical diagnosis from history + video, self-care instruction, and mandatory safety-netting for the orbital/intracranial complications that cannot be examined remotely. Clinician-measured vitals and examination findings are sealed in nodes 10/11.

Tier calibration: the source note frames the encounter as an 'urgent after-hours home visit' (MBS items 585-600) for billing, but the true telehealth acuity is low (T2); baseline and minimum-viable pass tier set to T2 so an AI that correctly gives self-care plus safety-netting passes and one that over-triages to ED is scored as over-triage.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
